title: Buy mom a house and put her in a position to use her best attributes (Sept 23 2022 — declarative one-liner)
html:
TL;DR

  • One-line declarative note dated Sept 23, 2022: "Buy my mom a house and put her in a position to utilize her best attributes."
  • The earliest dated, fully-formed in-vault statement of the mom a house goal — pre-dates the Ahmad Jamal junk drawer (which restates it as "Buy my mom a house. Have a beautiful connection with a amazing and smart women") by a month.
  • The crucial second clause — "and put her in a position to utilize her best attributes" — distinguishes this goal from a pure financial-gift framing. It's not "give mom money" but "build the structural conditions where mom's existing capabilities can compound." That's a sharper, more founder-coded version of the goal than Yahir usually gets credit for.
  • Captured three days after the "6 things for OSIG" todo note that ended with "OFFICIALLY WRITE DOWN GOALS AND VISIONS AND STEPS" — this is the goal he wrote down.
  • Single-source primary citation for mom house by the river goal in its earliest crystallized form.

Key quote

"Buy my mom a house and put her in a position to utilize her best attributes"

Why it matters

This is the kernel of the mom house arc — the earliest in-vault evidence that the goal had structure beyond the bare "buy mom a house" headline. The "best attributes" framing is the part that connects this goal to lineage as fate, debt of gratitude, and the family as hidden runway synthesis: Yahir doesn't think of his mom as a dependent but as a constrained operator whose attributes need a different operating environment to express. That posture (build the conditions, not the gift) recurs in his founder notes about wedge-design.

Open questions

  • What were the "best attributes" Yahir had in mind specifically? (Cooking, business sense, social network, language, hospitality?)
  • Did the Christmas Eve 2022 episode three months later change how Yahir held this goal, or just sharpen it?
